fact: June 8 is exactly 159 days into the year, which means there are only 206 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, the sun rises super early around June 8 - sometimes before 5:30 AM in places like New York!
Baby foxes are getting brave enough to leave their dens for the first time around this date, stumbling around like furry little drunken pirates.
June 8 falls smack in the middle of Gemini season, when people born under this zodiac sign are supposed to be extra chatty and curious.
Fireflies start blinking their butts like tiny disco balls in backyards across North America right around this time of year.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, June 8 is deep in winter, so penguins are huddling together for warmth while we're getting ready for summer!
The sun sets after 8 PM in many northern cities around June 8, giving kids extra daylight for playing outside after dinner.
Wild strawberries are getting plump and red in forests across Europe and North America, ready to be gobbled up by hungry bears and birds.
June 8 is only 13 days away from the summer solstice - the longest day of the year in the north!
Mosquitoes are at their most annoying around this date in many places because the weather is finally warm and wet enough for them to party.
In northern countries like Alaska, the sun barely sets at all around June 8, creating an eerie twilight that lasts almost all night long.
June 8 marks the time when many flowers release their strongest perfumes into the air, making bees go absolutely bonkers with excitement.
